title: Iran and US close to signing agreement on Strait of Hormuz
summary: President Trump announced that the signing of the agreement with Iran is scheduled for the 14th, including the opening of the Strait of Hormuz, although differences on nuclear issues persist.

title: G7 summit in Évian brings together world leaders with strict security measures
summary: The G7 summit began in Évian, France, with the participation of leaders of industrial powers to discuss trade, energy, and security, while Trump attends amid tensions.

title: High school student dies swept away by current in river in Nagoya
summary: A minor was swept away by the current while playing in a river in Nagoya and was found dead.

title: Mrs. Green Apple wins best artist award for second consecutive year
summary: The Japanese band Mrs. Green Apple was awarded the Best Artist prize at the 'Japan Grammy' ceremony for the second consecutive year.

title: Japanese Emperor and Empress begin official visit to Netherlands and Belgium
summary: Emperor Naruhito and Empress Masako began a visit to both countries after 24 years, with an agenda that includes meetings with the Dutch royal family.

title: United States defeats Paraguay in its World Cup debut
summary: The US team defeated Paraguay 2-0 with a double by Balogun in its first World Cup match.

title: SpaceX debuts on Nasdaq with valuation of 2.1 trillion dollars
summary: SpaceX went public reaching a market capitalization of 2.1 trillion dollars, making Elon Musk the first 'multi-trillionaire'.

title: Bear sightings in residential area of Nishinomiya generate alert
summary: Multiple sightings of a bear were reported in residential areas of Nishinomiya, possibly the same specimen.

title: DAZN apologizes for confusion over subscription plan prices
summary: DAZN offered apologies after some users mistakenly interpreted that the annual plan cost 980 yen per month.

title: Japan prepares to face Netherlands in World Cup
summary: The Japanese team studies tactics to counter the Dutch defense, considered one of the best in the world.

title: Tactical analysis of the Japanese national team for the World Cup
summary: Experts analyze Japan's strategy under Moriyasu's leadership, highlighting its evolution since the last World Cup.

title: England team suffers robbery before World Cup match
summary: The English team reported the theft of belongings, including possible soccer cleats, a few days before its debut.

title: Attack on tanker in Sea of Oman leaves three dead
summary: An attack on a tanker off Oman caused three fatalities, prompting a protest from India to the United States.

title: Japan rejects Trump's posts about anime
summary: The Japanese government expressed its disagreement with President Trump's posts about anime, defending its cultural industry.

title: Komeito seeks merger with the CRA before next parliamentary session
summary: The political party Komeito plans a merger with the Accounts Regulation Committee to strengthen its legislative influence.

title: British painter David Hockney dies at 88
summary: David Hockney, known for his vibrant California landscapes, died at the age of 88.

title: North Korea rejects joint declaration by South Korea and EU on nuclear weapons
summary: The North Korean Foreign Ministry criticized the joint declaration that does not recognize its status as a nuclear state.

title: Japan's urban baseball tournament continues with surprises
summary: The Intercity Baseball Tournament features exciting games, including the victory of Mitsubishi Heavy Industries East and the elimination of champion Yamaha.

title: Japanese local governments accumulate 102.5 billion yen in surplus from COVID-19 funds
summary: Japanese prefectures and municipalities kept spending low and generated a surplus of 102.5 billion yen from COVID-19 subsidies.

title: It is almost impossible to build a robot without China, according to analysis
summary: An article points out the global dependence on the Chinese supply chain for robot manufacturing.
